#### Reference issue
None.

#### What does this implement/fix?

Implement fast inverse hessian update formula, as suggested in the [BFGS Wikipedia article](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Broyden–Fletcher–Goldfarb–Shanno_algorithm):

![formula](https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/89eb8f4a521d630614dfc87e3a337d895fcb8a21)

#### Additional information
<!--Any additional information you think is important.-->

This PR consists of two commits, that are functionally equivalent:

1. First part implements the formula using only numpy routines. (Time complexity drops from $\mathcal{O}(N^3)$ to $\mathcal{O}(N^2)$; ~x5 speedup in a sample workload)
2. Second part use low-level BLAS routines for even faster symmetric matrix-vector multiplications. (Another ~x2 speedup in a sample workload)

If use of the low level routines (`symv`, `syr`, `syr2`) is not favored, reverting the second commit would suffice.

Link to the example benchmark is provided in the following gist for reference (python 3.12, numpy 1.26.4, scipy 1.14.1):

#### Reference issue
None.

#### What does this implement/fix?

Implement fast inverse hessian update formula, as suggested in the [BFGS Wikipedia article](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Broyden–Fletcher–Goldfarb–Shanno_algorithm):

![formula](https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/89eb8f4a521d630614dfc87e3a337d895fcb8a21)

#### Additional information
<!--Any additional information you think is important.-->

This PR consists of two commits, that are functionally equivalent:

1. First part implements the formula using only numpy routines. (Time complexity drops from $\mathcal{O}(N^3)$ to $\mathcal{O}(N^2)$; ~x5 speedup in a sample workload)
2. Second part use low-level BLAS routines for even faster symmetric matrix-vector multiplications. (Another ~x2 speedup in a sample workload)

If use of the low level routines (`symv`, `syr`, `syr2`) is not favored, reverting the second commit would suffice.

Link to the example benchmark is provided in the following gist for reference (python 3.12, numpy 1.26.4, scipy 1.14.1):
